strengths: Introduces structured claim-level memory with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Demonstrates explicit abstention policy as a behaviorally observable benefit
Ablation isolates LLM linker as the causal component for truth maintenance gains
Efficiency improvements over A-Mem with -62.1% token cost
weaknesses: Overall accuracy not statistically significant vs Flat RAG (p=0.21)
Abstention slice results underpowered (n=9) with trend-level significance
SSU slice (n=21) also underpowered with symmetric paired counts
Explicit abstention behavior doesn't improve measured accuracy on full abstention set (n=30)
required_revisions: Clarify why TTMG's explicit abstention policy matters despite equal outcome accuracy
Address low recall of intent classifier on multi-session questions (42.5%)
Explain handling of assistant-side claims that caused SSA/SSP regressions
Justify choice of MiniLM-L6 over stronger models for embedding

strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Explicit abstention policy via contradict/supersede edges, distinct from flat RAG's implicit handling
Ablation isolates LLM linker's causal role in truth maintenance
Efficiency gains over A-Mem with -62.1% token usage despite additional LLM calls
weaknesses: Overall accuracy not statistically distinguishable from Flat RAG (p=0.21)
Key capability slices (ABS, SSU) underpowered (n=9, n=21) for definitive claims
Structural regressions on assistant-authored and multi-session questions
Intent classifier for TTMG+R-LLM has low recall on MS/KU slices (42.5%/39.1%)
required_revisions: Report bootstrap CIs for all per-slice comparisons, not just overall accuracy
Clarify whether Kimi-K2 and DeepSeek-V3.2 are comparable in capability to avoid confounding
Address multi-session retrieval failure modes through multi-claim composition mechanisms
Justify stratified subset selection over full 500-question evaluation for stronger significance

strengths: Explicit truth-maintenance edges (contradict/supersede) enable structured consistency enforcement
Efficient session-batched writer and lexical-gated linker reduce compute cost by 62.1% vs A-Mem
Abstention policy is behaviorally observable and aligns with truth-maintenance goals
Ablation isolates LLM linker as the causal mechanism for gains on targeted slices
weaknesses: Overall accuracy falls short of Flat RAG (61.3% vs 68.0%) without statistical significance
Regressions on assistant-authored (SSA), preference (SSP), and multi-session (MS) slices
Intent classifier for TTMG+R-LLM has low recall on MS/KU slices (42.5%/39.1%)
Key paired results (ABS, SSU) operate at underpowered n=9 and n=21 sample sizes
required_revisions: Address structural limitations in assistant-authored and multi-session retrieval (F1-F3 in §5.2)
Improve intent classifier recall on MS/KU slices to close gap to oracle router
Clarify hybrid reader context integration mechanism (§4.1) and its impact on F1/F3 failures
Report bootstrap CIs for all slices, not just overall accuracy

strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Explicit abstention mechanism that fires on 80% of abstention questions
Lower token cost (-62%) vs A-Mem while preserving accuracy
Clean ablation showing LLM linker is causal for gains on targeted slices
Router composition recovers overall accuracy while keeping TTMG behaviour
weaknesses: Overall accuracy statistically ties Flat RAG (61.3 vs 68.0%, p=0.21) despite targeted wins
Small-sample significance: abstention slice n=9, SSU n=21; McNemar p>0.05 on both
Regressions on SSA/SSP/MS slices (-9.2 to -33 pp) limit standalone utility
Intent classifier recall low on MS/KU (42.5/39.1%) leaving 12-15 pp gap to oracle
Writer under-extracts assistant claims (7/7 SSA errors) and multi-claim aggregations
required_revisions: Run power analysis and scale evaluation to at least n≈50 per targeted slice for McNemar significance
Add per-question abstention detector to router so ABS accuracy equals TTMG, not Flat
Ablate writer density (assistant-side extraction) and multi-claim reader prompt to close F1–F3 failure modes
Report confidence-adjusted McNemar or bootstrap CI for the 4-0 abstention paired win
Clarify whether the 82% oracle upper bound is achievable with a better classifier or requires architectural change

strengths: Novel claim-level schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Ablation shows LLM linker is causal for gains on abstention (-22 pp) and knowledge-update (-9 pp)
Router composition recovers Flat RAG accuracy while preserving abstention behaviour
62 % token reduction vs A-Mem with comparable latency
Full reproducibility package: code, prompts, seeds, per-question outputs
weaknesses: Overall accuracy 61.3 % vs Flat 68.0 % is non-significant (p=0.21) and under-powered
Abstention slice n=9 gives only trend-level significance (p=0.125 two-sided, 4-0 paired)
Intent classifier recall 42.5 % on multi-session leaves 12-15 pp on table vs oracle
No cross-validation or multiple seeds; single stratified split (seed=0)
Failure-mode taxonomy (F1-F3) is post-hoc; no quantitative prevalence or fix validation
required_revisions: Run power analysis and scale evaluation to at least n≈200 for McNemar b-c≥6
Report cross-validation or bootstrap across multiple seeds to show stability
Quantify F1-F3 failure prevalence and test proposed fixes (denser assistant extraction, multi-claim reader)
Release statistical-test script and exact bootstrap code for reproducibility
Add error bars or CIs to per-slice accuracies in Table 3

strengths: Claim-level schema with validity intervals and explicit contradict/supersede edges is a clean abstraction for truth maintenance
Session-batched writer + lexical gate keeps linker cost low (-62 % tokens vs A-Mem)
Explicit abstention policy is behaviourally measurable and gives 80 % abstain rate on the probe set
Open-source release of prompts, code, and stratified splits aids reproducibility
Router composition recovers Flat RAG accuracy while preserving TTMG behaviour when triggered
weaknesses: Overall accuracy is 6.7 pp below Flat RAG on the primary 150-Q slice and not statistically distinguishable
Paired wins on abstention (n=9) and SSU (n=21) are under-powered (p=0.125 and p=0.50) and shrink to parity on the full 30-Q abstention set
Regressions on SSA/SSP/MS slices (-35 pp combined) show claim atom is too narrow for assistant turns, preferences, and multi-claim reasoning
Intent classifier recall on MS/KU is 39–43 %, leaving 12–15 pp on the table vs the oracle upper bound
No latency breakdown for linker vs reader; 85 s end-to-end is still too slow for interactive use
Hyper-parameters (τ_sim, τ_hard, k_keep) are hand-tuned on the same slice used for testing
Evaluation limited to one benchmark (LongMemEval-S) and one reader model (DeepSeek-V3.2)
required_revisions: Run power analysis and enlarge evaluation to at least 300–500 questions to confirm abstention/SSU gains hold
Add latency breakdown: writer, linker, parser, reader per question and wall-clock with 95 % CI
Abstain router must preserve TTMG abstention accuracy; currently falls back to Flat RAG and loses the 88.9 % ABS score
Report sensitivity of τ_sim, τ_hard, k_keep on a held-out dev slice; justify fixed values or provide adaptive rules
Evaluate on a second long-context benchmark (LoCoMo or MemoryAgentBench) to show generalisation
Include token cost per session-ingestion (not just per question) to clarify amortisation assumptions
Provide reader latency with a faster model (e.g., 7B-class) to show path to ≤10 s interactive use

strengths: Novel application of truth maintenance (contradict/supersede edges) to LLM agent memory, extending A-Mem's note graph to claim-level granularity
Well-designed ablation study isolating the LLM linker's causal contribution across all measured slices
Honest evaluation reporting non-significant results, underpowered tests, and detailed failure mode taxonomy (F1-F3)
Clear efficiency gains over A-Mem baseline (-62.1% tokens) with session-batched writing
Explicit abstention mechanism is architecturally clean and behaviorally observable (80% vs 0%)
Comprehensive appendix with prompts, hyperparameters, and reproducibility statement
weaknesses: Main headline result shows numerical regression (61.3% vs 68.0%) that fails to reach statistical significance; TTMG does not clearly outperform Flat RAG on overall accuracy
Key abstention claim on n=9 slice is underpowered (p=0.125 two-sided); on full n=30 abstention set, both systems tie at 76.7%
SSU improvement (n=21, p=0.50) is also underpowered; no statistically significant wins reported
Regressions on SSA, SSP, and MS slices require router composition to recover baseline performance
The primary behavioral 'win' (explicit abstention) does not translate to measurable accuracy gains over Flat RAG
Oracle upper bound (82.0%) shows substantial unexploited complementarity between claim-level and raw-text retrieval
required_revisions: Increase sample size beyond n=150 to achieve adequate statistical power for the claimed capability-slice improvements; current McNemar tests require b-c >= 6 for significance at alpha=0.05
Report effect sizes alongside p-values to quantify practical significance of differences
Clarify whether the explicit abstention behavior provides downstream value (e.g., user trust, error reduction) beyond the benchmark's accuracy metric
Address F1-F3 failure modes (denser assistant-side extraction, multi-claim composition) to strengthen the standalone TTMG case before router composition
Consider evaluating on additional benchmarks (LoCoMo, MemoryAgentBench) to demonstrate generalization of truth maintenance benefits

strengths: Proper paired statistical testing (McNemar) with exact p-values and bootstrap CIs
Honest reporting of statistical power limitations and underpowered comparisons
Comprehensive failure mode taxonomy (F1-F3) explaining regressions on SSA/SSP/MS slices
Ablation study isolating LLM linker's causal role with per-slice breakdown
Efficiency comparison showing -62.1% token reduction vs A-Mem baseline
Practical router composition (TTMG-Abstain, TTMG+R-LLM) for deployment
Detailed reproducibility statement with seeds, prompts, and model identifiers
weaknesses: Key capability slices are underpowered: abstention n=9 (p=0.125), SSU n=21 with paired 2-0 (p=0.50)
Overall accuracy regresses 6.7pp (61.3% vs 68.0%) - 'statistical parity' claim masks directional loss
A-Mem baseline is a reimplementation with missing per-slice data (-- entries in Table 1)
Router rows in Table 1 have missing per-slice breakdowns (-- for most columns)
Limited ablation coverage: only --contradict tested, no schema-only, threshold sensitivity, or k ablations
Intent classifier has low recall on key slices (42.5% MS, 39.1% KU) limiting router effectiveness
No comparison to other graph-based memory systems (MemGPT, MemoryBank) beyond A-Mem
Full n=30 abstention evaluation shows no accuracy advantage (both 76.7%), only behavioral difference
required_revisions: Report full n=500 LongMemEval-S results or justify why n=150 stratified slice is sufficient
Fill missing A-Mem per-slice results in Table 1 or explain why they cannot be computed
Add per-slice breakdown for router variants (TTMG-Abstain, TTMG+R-LLM) in Table 1
Add ablation for claim schema alone (no linker) to isolate schema contribution
Validate A-Mem reimplementation against original paper's reported numbers if available
Report standard errors or CIs on per-slice accuracies, not just overall
Consider one-sided McNemar for directional hypotheses (TTMG should not regress)

strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit truth-maintenance edges (contradict/supersede) addresses real gap in agent memory systems
Strong efficiency results: -62.1% token cost vs A-Mem (13603 vs 35904 tokens/question), 85s vs 117.1s latency
Session-batched writer amortizes LLM costs; subject-predicate lexical gate keeps linker off hot path for most claims
Thorough ablation isolating linker's causal role; honest failure mode taxonomy (F1-F3) explains regressions
Explicit abstention mechanism (80% on abstention questions vs 0% for Flat) is architecturally clean and observable
weaknesses: Overall accuracy 61.3% is numerically lower than Flat RAG 68.0% (p=0.21, not significant but directionally wrong)
On full n=30 abstention set, TTMG ties Flat RAG at 76.7% (p=1.00) - abstention benefit is behavioral, not accuracy
Regresses on SSA (7/7), SSP (3/3), MS (8/8) slices; requires router composition to match baseline overall
Router composition adds inference-time complexity and intent classifier (64.7% accuracy) becomes single point of failure
A-Mem row in Table 1 has missing values (-- for SSA/SSP/SSU); incomplete baseline comparison
Oracle gap (82.0% vs 67.3%) shows claim-level and raw-text retrieval are complementary but paper doesn't close this gap
required_revisions: Complete A-Mem baseline numbers for SSA, SSP, SSU slices in Table 1
Report confidence intervals for A-Mem overall accuracy (currently [--])
Clarify whether the 3-session ingestion cap (Appendix) affects fairness of comparison to baselines
Add analysis of linker invocation rate and per-question write-time breakdown to support efficiency claims
